title: Wire ledger writer to in-process queue
description: Foundation for ledger durability. event-ledger.ts:35 currently calls fs.appendFile directly — replace with createLedgerWriteQueue (from TASK-001). Single import + single line change automatically covers all 7 transitive append callsites: plan-context.ts:126, rule-sections.ts:191, read-ledger.ts:99 (worst-case fan-out), rule-meta-builder.ts:638/648/655, http.ts:64, audit-log.ts. Queue ensures ordering across in-process callers; fsyncgate handled separately by R3 signal handler shutdown.

files.change: Replace fs.appendFile at line 35 with createLedgerWriteQueue from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node/atomic-write. Initialize queue at module scope (singleton). All 12 callers automatically benefit.
Add test: concurrent appends from 5 producers serialize to ledger in submission order; 1 failed append doesn't poison subsequent ones

criteria: All ledger writers route through createLedgerWriteQueue
Concurrent append test asserts in-process serialization order
Existing pnpm test green (no regression in 12 callsites)
